title: Israel turns its back on France as Paris struggles to maintain dialogue
subhead: The Israeli Ministry of Defense announced on Tuesday that it would end its defense imports from France, implying that Paris was no longer counted among the country's allies. This move comes despite French hopes of repairing diplomatic ties damaged by France's recognition of the State of Palestine and amid the ongoing war in Lebanon.

title: Pesticides and cancer: For the first time, researchers find a connection at the national level
Israeli army says it 'struck senior Hezbollah commander' in Beirut
Palestinians voice anxiety and anger over Israeli law introducing death penalty
subhead: The law introducing the death penalty for Palestinians accused of deadly anti-Israeli attacks, condemned by Palestinian authorities and human rights organizations, has sparked a strong backlash. A call for a general strike was issued on Wednesday in East Jerusalem and the West Bank.

title: French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship are now available online
subhead: From March 30, over 180,000 records are accessible on the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons' website, due to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of people's 40 years of exile in France.

title: France's far right splits over the question of sovereignty as several mayors take down EU flags
subhead: Several newly elected mayors from far-right leader Marine Le Pen's party have removed the European Union flag, blue with gold stars, from their town halls. The decision has sparked divisions between the nationalists.

title: Europeans remain powerless to tackle Orban, seen as too close to the Kremlin
subhead: With Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has close ties with the Kremlin, vetoing new aid to Ukraine, European leaders hope their relations with Budapest will return to normal after Hungary's April 12 parliamentary elections.
byline: Jean-Baptiste Chastand,  Philippe Jacqué and  Virginie Malingre

Spice up your life! Five Paris restaurants where you can eat spicy food
subhead: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.

title: The French cadets suspected of setting a boat ablaze in Japan
subhead: An investigation is underway into how four cadets from France's elite Saint-Cyr military academy came to be accused of setting a boat on fire while on leave in Okinawa in 2024. The incident has fueled local distrust of military personnel on the island.

title: Céline Dion picks Paris for her return to the stage after a six-year hiatus
subhead: After several days of hints, the Canadian star waited for her birthday to announce her first concert dates since 2020: She will be in residence for 10 nights at Paris La Défense Arena, between September 12 and October 14.
